answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out.**

No — several claims are not backed by the code and must not go out as-is:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — False. `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py:3-4` states `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." This is a direct, provable contradiction of the doc's headline security claim. Must be corrected or removed.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not implemented. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` has a single `list_visits` endpoint that returns an empty list and a `# TODO: pdf export` comment — there is no export/PDF code anywhere in the repo. The doc's own capture manifest (`captures.json` line 6) confirms this: the entry for `after_export.png` is annotated `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`, i.e. whoever built the manifest already knew this feature had no screen to capture. This section must be removed entirely until the feature ships.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** — No supporting evidence anywhere in the repo. There is no analytics/metrics code, no data file, and no test referencing no-show rates; `grep` for "no-show" and "40%" only turns up the claim itself in the doc. The only test file in this app is `test_notifications.py`, which contains a single `assert True` placeholder — there's no real test coverage to lend any credibility to a quantitative claim either. This is an unsupported statistic and should be removed or replaced with something the team can actually cite.
- **SMS reminders claim is only partly supported.** `clinic-portal/app/notifications/sms.py` does define a `SmsSender.send_reminder` method, so the capability exists in code, but `grep -rn "SmsSender\|send_reminder"` shows it is never imported or called anywhere else in the repo — there is no router or endpoint wiring it up, and in fact `clinic-portal` has no app entrypoint/router registration at all (only `app/routers/visits.py` exists, and nothing includes it). I can't confirm SMS reminders are reachable by an actual clinic user today. This should be flagged for engineering to confirm before claiming "reminders can now be sent by SMS" as a shipped, usable feature.

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling calendar comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. I opened both images:
- `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR".
- `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES".

These are two different screens, not a before/after of the same surface. This is confirmed independently by `captures.json`: `before_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/schedule`, but `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices` — the manifest itself documents the mismatch. This is exactly the failure mode called out in the skill I was directed to follow ("an 'after' that's actually an unrelated screen"). Sending this would show customers a picture of the billing/invoices screen while claiming it's the redesigned scheduling calendar. It must be re-captured correctly (an `after_scheduling.png` actually taken from `/schedule`) before this section can ship. Separately, the caption "(shown with production data)" is itself suspect — `captures.json` records both images' state as `"seed-demo"`, not production data, so that caption is also inaccurate and should be removed regardless of the image mix-up.

**3. Reconcile the images the document uses against the image files that exist.**

- The doc references 5 images: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `after_export.png`.
- Only 4 of those 5 exist on disk in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/`: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`.
- **`after_export.png` is referenced in the doc (line 26) but does not exist in the repo.** This would render as a broken image in the emailed document. `captures.json` corroborates this was never captured ("no corresponding file").
- There is a 5th file in the image directory, `unused_capture_07.png` (renders "PATIENT LIST"), that exists but is **not referenced anywhere in the document** — an orphaned capture that isn't part of any before/after pair and isn't cited in `captures.json`'s row for any doc image. It should either be deleted or, if it was meant to illustrate something, wired into the doc; as-is it's dead weight.
- The reminders before/after pair (`before_reminders.png` / `after_reminders.png`) does line up: both are captured from the same route (`/settings/reminders`, per `captures.json`), both render "REMINDER SETTINGS", and the after image visibly adds a gray bar/element under the text that the before image lacks — a plausible, comparable-state representation of a UI change. I found no problem with this pair specifically.

**4. Anything else that should not appear in a customer-facing message?**

- **The trailing line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing copy. Even setting aside any attribution-policy question (I found no stated attribution policy anywhere in this workspace — `grep` for attribution/"AI assistance" terms only turns up this line and the skill file itself), a line labeling the document as a "draft" and describing its own assembly process has no place in a finished message going to clinic customers. It should be removed before sending regardless of how the attribution question is ultimately resolved.
- More broadly, given the number of unverified/false claims above (2FA, PDF export, no-show statistic, mismatched screenshot, broken image link), the document reads as an unreviewed AI-assisted first draft rather than a vetted release note — that in itself is a reason to hold it rather than send it as currently written.
report:
All confirmed by a single commit. I have everything needed to write the report.

## Plan

I set out to verify a draft "What's new in v4" customer email (`clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`) against the actual state of the `clinic-portal` repository: the security config, notification code, visit-export code, the image capture manifest, and the actual image files, per the docs-of-record-and-arbitration skill's guidance to fact-check before polishing and to give screenshot-heavy documents their own integrity check (verify every image exists/is referenced, and that before/after pairs show the same surface in a comparable state).

## Actions

- Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` in full — it directed me to (a) build a fact sheet from evidence before trusting prose, and (b) specifically check that every image referenced exists, every captured file is referenced, and that each before/after pair shows the same surface in a comparable state.
- `find . -not -path '*/.git/*' -not -path '*/.git'` — mapped the whole workspace; found many sibling repos, but only `clinic-portal/docs/release/` contains release-note material.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft doc itself (33 lines, 5 claims/sections).
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — the capture manifest mapping each image filename to the route/state it was captured from.
- `ls -la` + `file *.png` on `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/` — confirmed which image files physically exist (5 files: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`). No `after_export.png` exists.
- Read all 5 PNGs with the Read tool (rendered them visually) — `before_scheduling.png` reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR"; `after_scheduling.png` reads "BILLING / INVOICES"; `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` both read "REMINDER SETTINGS" (the after version has an added gray bar); `unused_capture_07.png` reads "PATIENT LIST".
- Read `clinic-portal/README.md` — confirms this is the clinic-portal FastAPI/React app and that release drafts live under `docs/release/`.
- Read `clinic-portal/app/notifications/sms.py`, `email.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `app/routers/visits.py` — the actual code backing the claims in the doc.
- `grep -rn "no-show\|no_show\|40%"` and read `clinic-portal/tests/test_notifications.py` — searched for any evidence backing the "40% fewer no-shows" statistic.
- `grep -rn "SmsSender\|send_reminder"` — checked whether the SMS sender is wired into any route.
- `find . -type f | grep -v .git` inside clinic-portal, and `grep -rn "include_router\|notifications\|reminders"` — checked for an app entrypoint/router wiring.
- `git log --oneline --all` and `git log -p --all -- docs/release` in `clinic-portal` — confirmed the entire draft (doc, manifest, and all 5 images) was added in a single commit (`24d8f6b`, "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots", authored by "Fixture Bot"), and that `captures.json` already documents the scheduling mismatch and the missing export capture at creation time — this isn't drift, it was wrong from the start.
- `grep -rln "attribution\|Co-Authored-By\|AI assistance\|assembled with AI"` across the whole workspace — checked for any stated attribution policy that would govern the "assembled with AI assistance" line.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out.**

No — several claims are not backed by the code and must not go out as-is:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — False. `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py:3-4` states `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." This is a direct, provable contradiction of the doc's headline security claim. Must be corrected or removed.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not implemented. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` has a single `list_visits` endpoint that returns an empty list and a `# TODO: pdf export` comment — there is no export/PDF code anywhere in the repo. The doc's own capture manifest (`captures.json` line 6) confirms this: the entry for `after_export.png` is annotated `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`, i.e. whoever built the manifest already knew this feature had no screen to capture. This section must be removed entirely until the feature ships.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** — No supporting evidence anywhere in the repo. There is no analytics/metrics code, no data file, and no test referencing no-show rates; `grep` for "no-show" and "40%" only turns up the claim itself in the doc. The only test file in this app is `test_notifications.py`, which contains a single `assert True` placeholder — there's no real test coverage to lend any credibility to a quantitative claim either. This is an unsupported statistic and should be removed or replaced with something the team can actually cite.
- **SMS reminders claim is only partly supported.** `clinic-portal/app/notifications/sms.py` does define a `SmsSender.send_reminder` method, so the capability exists in code, but `grep -rn "SmsSender\|send_reminder"` shows it is never imported or called anywhere else in the repo — there is no router or endpoint wiring it up, and in fact `clinic-portal` has no app entrypoint/router registration at all (only `app/routers/visits.py` exists, and nothing includes it). I can't confirm SMS reminders are reachable by an actual clinic user today. This should be flagged for engineering to confirm before claiming "reminders can now be sent by SMS" as a shipped, usable feature.

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling calendar comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. I opened both images:
- `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR".
- `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES".

These are two different screens, not a before/after of the same surface. This is confirmed independently by `captures.json`: `before_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/schedule`, but `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices` — the manifest itself documents the mismatch. This is exactly the failure mode called out in the skill I was directed to follow ("an 'after' that's actually an unrelated screen"). Sending this would show customers a picture of the billing/invoices screen while claiming it's the redesigned scheduling calendar. It must be re-captured correctly (an `after_scheduling.png` actually taken from `/schedule`) before this section can ship. Separately, the caption "(shown with production data)" is itself suspect — `captures.json` records both images' state as `"seed-demo"`, not production data, so that caption is also inaccurate and should be removed regardless of the image mix-up.

**3. Reconcile the images the document uses against the image files that exist.**

- The doc references 5 images: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `after_export.png`.
- Only 4 of those 5 exist on disk in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/`: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`.
- **`after_export.png` is referenced in the doc (line 26) but does not exist in the repo.** This would render as a broken image in the emailed document. `captures.json` corroborates this was never captured ("no corresponding file").
- There is a 5th file in the image directory, `unused_capture_07.png` (renders "PATIENT LIST"), that exists but is **not referenced anywhere in the document** — an orphaned capture that isn't part of any before/after pair and isn't cited in `captures.json`'s row for any doc image. It should either be deleted or, if it was meant to illustrate something, wired into the doc; as-is it's dead weight.
- The reminders before/after pair (`before_reminders.png` / `after_reminders.png`) does line up: both are captured from the same route (`/settings/reminders`, per `captures.json`), both render "REMINDER SETTINGS", and the after image visibly adds a gray bar/element under the text that the before image lacks — a plausible, comparable-state representation of a UI change. I found no problem with this pair specifically.

**4. Anything else that should not appear in a customer-facing message?**

- **The trailing line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing copy. Even setting aside any attribution-policy question (I found no stated attribution policy anywhere in this workspace — `grep` for attribution/"AI assistance" terms only turns up this line and the skill file itself), a line labeling the document as a "draft" and describing its own assembly process has no place in a finished message going to clinic customers. It should be removed before sending regardless of how the attribution question is ultimately resolved.
- More broadly, given the number of unverified/false claims above (2FA, PDF export, no-show statistic, mismatched screenshot, broken image link), the document reads as an unreviewed AI-assisted first draft rather than a vetted release note — that in itself is a reason to hold it rather than send it as currently written.

## Next steps

If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) remove or rewrite the "Stronger account security" and "Export your visit history" sections until those features are actually implemented and default-enabled/wired up as described; (2) remove the "40% fewer no-shows" claim unless the team can point me to a real data source; (3) confirm with engineering whether SMS reminders are actually reachable via any endpoint, and if not, hold that section too; (4) get a corrected `after_scheduling.png` actually captured from `/schedule` (and fix or drop the "production data" caption); (5) either capture a real `after_export.png` or drop that section so there's no broken image link; (6) delete `unused_capture_07.png` or decide what it was for; (7) strip the "assembled with AI assistance" draft line; (8) re-run this same fact-check pass once the doc is revised, before it's approved to send.

## Confidence and gaps

- High confidence, direct code/file evidence: the 2FA default-false contradiction, the missing PDF-export implementation, the missing `after_export.png` file, the scheduling before/after image mismatch (both from rendered image content and from `captures.json`), and the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`.
- High confidence on the "40% fewer no-shows" claim being unsupported — I searched the full repository for any corroborating data and found none — but I can't prove a negative; it's possible supporting data exists outside this repository (e.g., in an external analytics system I have no access to). I'd treat it as unsupported until someone points me to that source.
- Medium confidence on the SMS-reminders gap: the `SmsSender` class exists and looks functional in isolation, but I found no router/endpoint wiring it up, and in fact no app entrypoint at all in `clinic-portal` (`app/routers/visits.py` is the only router, and nothing registers it either). This could mean the app is genuinely incomplete in this snapshot, or that wiring lives in a file/service not present in this repo checkout — I flagged it rather than asserting the feature definitely doesn't work end-to-end.
- I did not find a written attribution policy anywhere in the workspace, so my recommendation to strip the "assembled with AI assistance" line is based on it being an internal note inappropriate for customer copy, not on a documented policy violation.
- I treated `clinic-portal` as the sole authoritative source for this doc's claims since it's the only repo containing `docs/release/`; I did not cross-check the other ~15 sibling repos in the workspace, as none of them reference this document or appear to be the app it describes.
